validation_output: Read-only guardrail review completed. No files edited. No broad verification commands run, per packet. Inspected only the requested feature artifacts and scoped source files with narrow in-file symbol searches.
consumer_evidence: Guardrail review consumed feature plan, data model, API contract, provider service, and Settings UI references before implementation tasks.

boundary_surfaces_confirmed: src/server/services/providerService.ts owns provider persistence via providers.json, provider CRUD, activeId, activation, and managed settings sync.
desktop/src/pages/Settings.tsx owns the Settings > Providers UI surface and currently contains provider CRUD, activation, testing, and provider-form settings JSON behavior.
mp_obligations_recognized: MP-001 through MP-010 must be preserved: provider sharing goal, saved-provider-only scope, separate secret-free and secret export paths, server-side redaction, high-friction secret export, add/rename default conflicts, no active/default transfer, side-effect-free preview, workflow UI as interaction precedent only, and no frontend-only masking as export safety.
ca_obligations_recognized: CA-001 through CA-007 are downstream implementation obligations: read-only default export redaction, side-effect-free preview, commit without activation/env mutation, explicit conflict resolution, generated local IDs, redaction independent of list masking, and explicit per-request secret export confirmation.
forbidden_drift_downstream_must_preserve: Do not implement provider sharing as raw Settings JSON import/export.
Do not include activeId, default provider selection, official OAuth/login state, chat/session/plugin/skill/MCP/workflow state, or global app settings in provider bundles.
Do not silently overwrite existing providers.
Do not implement secret export as a remembered checkbox.
Do not rely on frontend apiKey masking for export safety.
Do not mutate ~/.claude/settings.json as part of provider import.
Do not transfer active/default provider selection during import or export.
Do not expose raw credentials in diagnostics, logs, previews, or default bundles.

concerns: Settings.tsx already has provider-form settings JSON masking/restoration helpers; downstream work must keep that behavior separate from the new provider-specific bundle model.
ProviderService currently syncs managed settings when active providers are updated or activated; import commit must avoid calling activation paths or active-provider settings sync.

ProviderService list/get/proxy paths can expose stored apiKey internally; default export must construct bundles server-side with explicit credential redaction rather than reusing raw provider objects blindly.
Project cognition remains unavailable because .specify/project-cognition/project-cognition.db is missing, so this review used live repository evidence and feature artifacts only.
recovery_hints: If downstream implementation needs more context, read the required implementation references listed in plan.md before editing, especially src/server/api/providers.ts, src/server/types/provider.ts, desktop/src/api/providers.ts, desktop/src/stores/providerStore.ts, and WorkflowImportExportDialog.tsx.
Keep import/export schema separate from persisted ProvidersIndex and from Settings JSON helpers.
Add server-side tests first for CA-001 through CA-006 before wiring desktop behavior; add desktop confirmation/preview tests for CA-007 and UI diagnostics.
